subject: red pandas
one_liner: Live streaming infrastructure for creators who are asleep seventeen hours a day.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan, metrics-forward, faux-corporate. Every joke is stated as an operational fact. The platform treats a solitary, unconscious, crepuscular animal as a top-tier creator segment and never acknowledges the problem. Incident reports, quarterly digests, and enclosure success managers are discussed the way a real trust-and-safety org would discuss them. Never winks. Never uses streamer slang enthusiastically; corporate has clearly sanitized it.

hero.sub: Wah is the streaming platform built around the red panda broadcast schedule: two short windows at dawn and dusk, and seventeen hours of premium unconscious content in between.

features.title: Unconscious Uptime
features.blurb: Streams stay live while the creator sleeps. The average Wah broadcast runs nine hours, of which the creator is awake for 41 minutes. Retention has never been higher, and we have stopped asking why.

features.title: Solo Raid Protocol
features.blurb: Red pandas are territorial and do not collaborate. If two creators appear in the same frame, both streams end immediately and an incident report is filed. Collabs are a stress event.

features.title: Chat, Contained
features.blurb: Viewers can type whatever they want. Messages are reviewed by a moderation team and released to the creator in a quarterly digest. In four years of operation, no creator has ever been startled.

pricing_tiers.name: Cub
pricing_tiers.tagline: For creators still living with their mother.

pricing_tiers.name: Arboreal
perks: Bamboo Bits payouts, settled in bamboo
One incident report waived per year

testimonials.quote: I streamed for six hours yesterday. I was asleep for all of it. Revenue is up 300 percent.
testimonials.name: Pabu
testimonials.title: Partnered Creator, Enclosure 4, Rotterdam Zoo

testimonials.quote: Wah ended my stream the second my brother entered the frame. Correct decision. Do not reinstate him.
testimonials.name: Miso
testimonials.title: Variety Streamer and Only Child

testimonials.quote: We moved our entire red panda program onto Wah. Engagement metrics stopped meaning anything, which frankly has been a relief.
testimonials.name: Deborah Ankiel
testimonials.title: Deputy Curator of Small Mammals and Interim Head of Talent, Meadowbrook Zoological Society

demo_screen_concept: A live stream viewer for the channel 'pabu_asleep': a 16:9 stream frame showing a red panda asleep on a branch at dusk with a LIVE badge and a viewer counter that slowly climbs on its own; a chat column in slow mode where anything the visitor types is immediately marked 'held for quarterly review'; a Bamboo Bits tipping row whose tips increment an on-screen bamboo counter and trigger a polite acknowledgment from no one; a follow button; and a sidebar of recommended channels, every one of them marked ASLEEP or OFFLINE. Clicking a recommended channel swaps the player to that channel's still and stats.

images.rationale: photo-shaped demo (a live stream player showing a red panda): 2 stream stills at 16:9, the main one shared between the landing hero player card and the demo player, the second for the demo channel switcher; 2 deadpan corporate headshots for the Pabu and Miso testimonials; standalone hero image skipped because the hero's only image surface is the player card itself; Deborah Ankiel (human) keeps the initials-circle treatment.

items.prompt: Editorial wildlife photograph, a single red panda fast asleep curled into a loaf on a thick tree branch, tail wrapped over its face, photographed at dusk. Deep dusk-plum twilight sky backdrop in the tone of #251A3A, warm ember rim light in the tone of #E8813C catching the fur from one side, faint cool lavender #B9A3E8 ambient fill. Cozy, serene, cinematic depth of field, one focal subject. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Editorial wildlife photograph, a single red panda half-awake mid-yawn on a mossy branch among dusk foliage, eyes heavy-lidded, photographed at twilight. Deep dusk-plum sky backdrop in the tone of #251A3A, warm ember rim light in the tone of #E8813C, soft cool lavender #B9A3E8 haze in the background leaves. One focal subject, cinematic depth of field, drowsy mood. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Deadpan corporate headshot photograph of a single red panda posed like a LinkedIn profile picture, facing the camera with a flat neutral expression, shoulders square, studio lighting. Muted dusk-plum studio backdrop in the tone of #251A3A with a soft lavender #B9A3E8 gradient, warm ember key light in the tone of #E8813C on the fur. Straight-faced, professional, one focal subject. The photograph must fill the entire square frame edge to edge, full bleed: no white borders, no letterboxing, no frame, no matting of any kind. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Deadpan corporate headshot photograph of a single red panda in three-quarter profile looking slightly off camera like an executive portrait, ears alert, unimpressed expression, studio lighting. Muted dusk-plum studio backdrop in the tone of #251A3A with a faint warm ember glow in the tone of #E8813C from one side, cool lavender #B9A3E8 fill light. Straight-faced, professional, one focal subject, visibly different pose and framing from a standard passport photo. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

design_direction.style: Glassmorphism, crepuscular dusk-broadcast mode. The whole page is staged at the only hour a red panda is awake: a deep dusk-plum sky ground carries a slow vertical gradient toward a faint ember horizon glow at the bottom edge, with two soft ambient light blobs (one warm ember low, one cool lavender high) drifting almost imperceptibly behind the content. Every panel is frosted glass: translucent surfaces at 8-14% white with backdrop-filter blur (14-18px), a 1px rgba-white hairline border, and generous 20px radii, so cards read as panes of cold evening air rather than paper. One ember rust-orange, the color of the animal itself, carries the wordmark, the LIVE badge, the single primary CTA per section, and every monetization affordance, and appears almost nowhere else; one dusk lavender handles links, tags, secondary chips, and the glass tint. Fredoka sets the wordmark and headlines in soft rounded medium-weight geometry that reads like a consumer streaming brand; Nunito carries calm rounded body copy. Every figure that pretends to be broadcast telemetry (viewer counts, hours asleep, Bamboo Bits balances, tier prices, uptime percentages) is set in Nunito with tabular figures inside small glass chips so it reads like a player overlay, not a marketing number. Motion is dusk-native and slow: the LIVE dot pulses on a 4s cycle, the viewer count ticks upward lazily, glass panels float up 8px and fade in on scroll, hover lifts a card 2px and warms its border, and everything is gated behind prefers-reduced-motion. No neon, no scanlines, no hard offset shadows, no paper grain, no ink keylines, no light mode, because the creators are crepuscular and the platform respects that. Reads like a heavily funded consumer streaming platform that has accepted, at the board level, that its entire creator class is unconscious.
design_direction.archetype: Consumer streaming-platform landing: hero split with copy left and a frosted live-player mockup right, three feature glass cards, testimonial row with headshots, three-tier pricing, footer. Demo is a stream-viewer product UI with player, chat rail, and channel sidebar.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Slow dusk motion: 4s pulsing LIVE dot, lazily ticking viewer counter, glass panels floating up 8px with 300ms ease-out on scroll reveal, 2px hover lift with warmed border, ambient gradient blobs drifting on a 20s cycle. All gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Dusk gradient sky with ember glow pooling at the bottom edge. Left column: wordmark, oversized Fredoka headline, sub, single ember CTA. Right column: a frosted glass stream-player card for 'pabu_asleep' showing a sleeping red panda still with a pulsing LIVE badge, a climbing viewer count chip, and a muted 'creator asleep' status row. The player card is the only image-bearing surface in the hero.
